headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Surges 1,200 Points As Fed Minutes Loom; Alphabet Soars (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes held the bulk of their hefty gains Wednesday afternoon after President Donald Trump suspended Iran attacks for two weeks while Tehran agreed to open the Strait of Hormuz.  Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve's minutes from the March rate-setting meeting are due on the stock market today.  In early afternoon trade, the Dow Jones Industrial Average jumped 2.7%, or more than 1,200 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.

Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve's minutes from the March rate-setting meeting are due on the stock market today.  In early afternoon trade, the Dow Jones Industrial Average jumped 2.7%, or more than 1,200 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.

headline: Where Nvidia Stock Needs to Trade to Get Out of Its Sideways Trap
summary: Nvidia  stock is having a good day, but it’s still stuck in the purgatory of a nine-month trading range.  Nvidia stock was up 2% on Wednesday, after gaining 0.3% on Tuesday; the  has risen 2.5%, and the  has advanced 2.7%.  The stock was down throughout the day on Tuesday after reports that the U.S. had attacked military targets on Kharg Island, from which much of Iran’s oil is exported.
